openmeter/billing/models/creditsapplied.purpose: Defines the CreditsApplied slice type and CreditApplied value type representing credits applied to a billing line item. Enforces that all credit amounts are positive and provides currency-precision-aware summation.

patterns.name: models.Validator interface compliance
patterns.description: Both CreditsApplied and CreditApplied implement models.Validator via a Validate() error method. All new types here must do the same and be verified with a compile-time assertion: var _ models.Validator = (*T)(nil).

patterns.name: models.Clonable interface compliance
patterns.description: CreditsApplied implements models.Clonable[CreditsApplied] with a Clone() method using lo.Map for safe copy. Any slice or pointer field added to this package must be deep-copied in Clone.
patterns.example: func (c CreditsApplied) Clone() CreditsApplied { return lo.Map(c, func(item CreditApplied, _ int) CreditApplied { return item }) }

patterns.name: Currency-aware summation
patterns.description: SumAmount rounds each item via currencyx.Calculator.RoundToPrecision before accumulating. New aggregation helpers must use the calculator for rounding, not raw Add.

key_file_guides.role: Single source of all types in this package. Defines CreditsApplied, CreditApplied, and all methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreditApplied.Amount must remain positive (validated). Adding nullable fields requires updating Clone and Validate.

anti_patterns: Storing negative credit amounts — Validate() enforces positivity; bypassing it causes billing arithmetic errors.
Accumulating amounts without RoundToPrecision — causes currency precision drift across line items.
Adding DB/Ent concerns to this package — it is a pure domain model with no persistence code.

decisions.decision: CreditsApplied is a named slice type rather than a struct with a slice field.
decisions.rationale: Allows direct iteration, len checks, and method attachment without wrapping, matching the pattern used by other billing model collections.

openmeter/billing/models/externalid.purpose: Defines external-ID model types (InvoiceExternalIDs, LineExternalIDs) and generic Ent mixin/helper functions for reading, writing, and mapping external app IDs (invoicing, payment) on invoice and line Ent entities.

patterns.name: Typed Creator/Updater/Getter generic interfaces
patterns.description: Each Ent operation (create, update, map-from-DB) has a corresponding generic interface (LineExternalIDCreator[T], LineExternalIDUpdater[T], LineExternalIDGetter). Adapters must use the provided helpers (CreateLineExternalID, UpdateLineExternalID, MapLineExternalIDFromDB) rather than calling Set* methods directly.
patterns.example: func CreateLineExternalID[T LineExternalIDCreator[T]](creator LineExternalIDCreator[T], ids LineExternalIDs) T

patterns.name: lo.EmptyableToPtr for optional string fields
patterns.description: Empty strings are converted to nil pointers using lo.EmptyableToPtr when writing to Ent. New external-ID fields must follow this pattern to avoid storing empty strings in nullable DB columns.

patterns.name: Ent mixin for schema fields
patterns.description: LineMixin and InvoiceMixin embed mixin.Schema and declare optional/nillable string fields. Add new external-ID fields to the correct mixin (LineMixin vs InvoiceMixin), then add corresponding getter/setter to the typed interfaces.

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin schema definitions and generic helper functions for CRUD operations on external IDs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new external-ID field requires updating: (1) the Mixin Fields(), (2) the Creator/Updater/Getter interfaces, (3) the Create/Update/Map helpers, and (4) the model.go struct.

key_file_guides.role: Pure domain structs InvoiceExternalIDs and LineExternalIDs with nil-safe accessor methods and equality check.
key_file_guides.watch_for: LineExternalIDs.Equal must be updated when new fields are added.

anti_patterns: Storing empty strings in nullable external-ID columns — always use lo.EmptyableToPtr.
Calling Set* Ent methods directly in adapters instead of using the typed helpers — breaks consistency when fields are added.
Adding business logic to this package — it is purely a model/mixin utility layer.

decisions.decision: Generic interfaces (Creator[T], Updater[T], Getter) rather than concrete Ent types.
decisions.rationale: Both invoice and line Ent entities share the same external-ID fields; generics allow a single helper to work across both entity types without duplication.

openmeter/billing/models/stddetailedline.purpose: Defines the Base struct for standard detailed billing line items, along with Ent mixin, DB mapping helpers, generic Creator interface for Ent mutations, and goderive-generated equality. This is the shared schema/model layer consumed by both Ent adapters and domain services for flat-fee and usage-based detailed lines.

patterns.name: Composite Ent mixin via entutils.RecursiveMixin
patterns.description: Mixin embeds entutils.RecursiveMixin[mixinBase] which composes entutils.AnnotationsMixin, entutils.ResourceMixin, and totals.Mixin. New fields must be added to mixinBase.Fields(), not directly to entity schemas.

patterns.name: Generic Creator interface for Ent mutations
patterns.description: Creator[T] composes externalid.LineExternalIDCreator[T], billingtotals.Setter[T], and all primitive Set* methods. The Create[T] function uses this to atomically populate all fields. New fields require updating both Creator[T] and the Create function.
patterns.example: func Create[T Creator[T]](creator Creator[T], line Base) T

patterns.name: DBGetter interface for DB-to-domain mapping
patterns.description: FromDB[T DBGetter] reads all fields from a generic DB entity through the DBGetter interface. Adding a field to Base requires adding the corresponding getter to DBGetter and updating FromDB.
patterns.example: func FromDB[T DBGetter](dbEntity T, taxConfig *productcatalog.TaxConfig) Base

patterns.name: goderive equality generation
patterns.description: Base.Equal delegates to deriveEqualBase generated by goderive. After adding fields to Base, run `go generate` in this package to regenerate derived.gen.go. Never hand-edit derived.gen.go.
patterns.example: func (l Base) Equal(other Base) bool { return deriveEqualBase(&l, &other) }

patterns.name: models.Validator on enums and structs
patterns.description: Category implements models.Validator and uses slices.Contains against Category("").Values(). New enum types must follow the same Values()/Validate() pattern with compile-time assertion.

patterns.name: Deep clone for pointer/slice fields
patterns.description: Base.Clone explicitly deep-copies TaxConfig pointer and CreditsApplied slice. Any new pointer or slice field added to Base must be deep-copied in Clone.

key_file_guides.role: Canonical Base struct, Category enum, Comparable interface, Compare function, and BackfillTaxConfig helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding fields to Base requires updating: Clone, Equal (via goderive regen), DBGetter, Creator[T], FromDB, and mixin.go Fields().

key_file_guides.role: Ent schema mixin providing all DB fields for stddetailedline entities. Also declares SQL CHECK constraints and indexes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: child_unique_reference_id has a NOT EMPTY SQL CHECK — never allow it to be optional. credits_applied is jsonb-optional.

key_file_guides.role: Generic Create[T] function for populating Ent create mutations from a Base value.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must stay in sync with all Base fields; missing a field here silently omits it from DB writes.

key_file_guides.role: DBGetter interface and FromDB generic mapping function from DB rows to Base.
key_file_guides.watch_for: time.UTC normalization must be applied to all time fields via .In(time.UTC).

key_file_guides.role: Goderive-generated equality helpers. DO NOT EDIT — regenerate with go generate.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If Base gains fields with custom equality semantics (e.g. decimal), the generated code uses .Equal() if available, otherwise == — verify correctness after regen.
Run after any change to Base struct to keep derived.gen.go in sync.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing derived.gen.go — always regenerate via go generate after Base changes.
Adding DB/Ent logic directly to model.go or domain logic to mixin.go — keep the layers separate.
Omitting time.UTC normalization in FromDB for time.Time fields — causes timezone-dependent test failures.
Forgetting to update Creator[T] and DBGetter when adding a new field to Base — silently omits the field from writes/reads.
Making child_unique_reference_id optional — it has a DB CHECK constraint and is required for line upsert identity.

decisions.decision: Base struct lives in the same package as the Ent mixin rather than in a separate domain package.
decisions.rationale: Both the Ent schema definition and the domain model must stay in lock-step; co-locating them makes drift immediately visible and allows the Creator/DBGetter interfaces to share the same package-level types.

decisions.decision: goderive for equality rather than hand-written Equal methods.
decisions.rationale: Base contains alpacadecimal.Decimal fields that require .Equal() not ==; goderive generates correct field-by-field equality automatically and stays in sync with struct changes via go generate.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new field to Base and wire it through all layers

openmeter/billing/models/totals.purpose: Defines the Totals struct representing a billing line's financial breakdown (amount, taxes, charges, discounts, credits, total) with currency-precision rounding, additive aggregation, and an Ent mixin for persisting all fields as PostgreSQL numeric columns.

patterns.name: Ent mixin with typed Setter/Getter interfaces
patterns.description: totals.Mixin declares all 8 numeric DB fields. totals.Setter[T] and totals.TotalsGetter provide typed interfaces for Ent create/update mutations and DB reads. Use Set[T](mut, totals) and FromDB(e) for all Ent interactions — never call individual Set*/Get* methods directly.
patterns.example: func Set[T Setter[T]](mut Setter[T], totals Totals) T { return mut.SetAmount(totals.Amount). /* chain all 8 fields */ }

patterns.name: Currency-precision rounding via currencyx.Calculator
patterns.description: RoundToPrecision applies calculator.RoundToPrecision to every field atomically. Always call this before persisting or comparing totals across currencies.

patterns.name: Additive aggregation via Add/Sum
patterns.description: Totals.Add accumulates multiple Totals values field-by-field. Use Sum(...) to aggregate a variadic list. Do not accumulate individual fields outside these helpers.

patterns.name: Validate enforces non-negative invariants
patterns.description: All 8 fields must be non-negative. Validate() is called in the billing domain before persistence. New fields added to Totals must have a corresponding non-negative check in Validate.
patterns.example: if t.Amount.IsNegative() { return errors.New("amount is negative") }

key_file_guides.role: Totals struct with Validate, Add, Sum, RoundToPrecision, and CalculateTotal methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CalculateTotal formula: Amount + ChargesTotal + TaxesExclusiveTotal - DiscountsTotal - CreditsTotal. Adding a new field requires updating Add, RoundToPrecision, Validate, and CalculateTotal as appropriate.

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin (Mixin struct) + Setter[T]/TotalsGetter interfaces + Set[T] and FromDB helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All 8 fields use dialect.Postgres: 'numeric'. Adding a field requires updating Fields(), Setter[T], TotalsGetter, Set[T], and FromDB in lockstep.

key_file_guides.role: Unit test for RoundToPrecision with USD half-up rounding semantics.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use exact string comparison on rounded decimals — update expected values if precision logic changes.

anti_patterns: Directly calling individual Set*/Get* Ent methods for totals fields — always use Set[T] and FromDB helpers for consistency.
Accumulating totals outside Add/Sum — leads to fields being missed.
Skipping RoundToPrecision before persistence — causes numeric precision drift in multi-currency invoices.
Adding a field to Totals without updating Add, RoundToPrecision, Validate, mixin Fields(), Setter[T], TotalsGetter, Set[T], and FromDB.

decisions.decision: All totals fields use alpacadecimal.Decimal stored as PostgreSQL numeric.
decisions.rationale: Billing requires exact decimal arithmetic; float64 SQL types would introduce rounding errors at the storage layer.

decisions.decision: Mixin and domain model co-located in the same package.
decisions.rationale: Ensures the Setter/Getter interfaces stay in sync with the Totals struct fields without cross-package indirection.
